newsitems.title: 50,000 Wishlists & Launch is Just One Week Away!
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️Ore Factory Squad launches in just one week, on July 16!And we’re incredibly excited to share that the game has now reached 50,000 wishlists on Steam! Thank you so much to everyone who has supported us along the way. 💙To celebrate the final countdown, here’s our brand-new trailer, featuring a closer look at everything waiting for you in the full game!At launch; You’ll be able to expand your operation across 4 different dig sites, each offering new environments, underground dangers, and resources to discover.Starting with basic materials like stone and working your way up to Gold and even Diamond, you’ll collect and produce more than 60 resources and products across 3 main production lines.You’ll also be able to hire Miner and Handler employees, send workers on off-site contracts, unlock a wide range of upgrades and new systems, explore city activities, expand your vehicle fleet, and build your factory alone or with your squad! There’s only one week left until you can begin your own mining and factory operation! Don’t forget to wishlist Ore Factory Squad so you don’t miss the launch on July 16! 🚀

newsitems.title: Devlog #2 | Expanding Beyond the Backyard
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️Ore Factory Squad is launching on July 16! Get ready to expand your factory across different dig sites, each with new resources to discover, unique environments to explore, and new challenges waiting underground along with many more features to try out! Make sure to wishlist the game so you don’t miss the launch!Before we jump into today’s devlog, we also want to say a huge thank you. Ore Factory Squad has now passed 45,000+ wishlists on Steam, and your support means a lot to us.In this devlog, we’ll take a closer look at the different maps you’ll unlock as you progress and what makes each dig site unique. Let’s get into it! Dig Sites at LaunchWith the launch version of Ore Factory Squad, there will be 4 different dig sites to unlock and purchase as you progress through the game.You already know the Suburban from the demo, so today we want to talk about two other locations you’ll be able to visit in the full game: Forest and Construction Site Forest – A Breath of Fresh AirThe Forest dig site is a quiet roadside property surrounded by green pine trees, fresh air, and a beautiful lake view.Compared to the starting area, Forest gives you a much larger digging space and more room to build your underground operation. But it’s not just bigger on the surface, there’s more waiting below as well.This is one of the first places where you’ll start encountering resources such as Copper, Bronze, Iron, and Steel, opening the way to more advanced production chains and new machines in your factory.Forest is designed to feel like the moment your mining business really starts expanding. You leave the backyard behind, move into a larger natural environment, and start chasing more serious resources. Underground CavesForest also introduces underground caves.These caves are small tunnel-like areas that are generated differently each time you purchase the property. They are not meant to be massive cave networks, but rather hidden pockets underground that reward exploration.Each cave entrance is blocked by a breakable wall. To enter, you’ll need to use dynamite and blast your way through.Inside these caves, you may find valuable materials that are harder to reach through regular digging. So while you can keep mining normally, players who explore can uncover extra rewards.Construction Site – Dust, Sand & CementThe Construction Site is a very different kind of dig site.While Redstone City has its old industrial roots, this area represents the city’s new expansion, a developing district filled with tall constructions, unfinished roads and the chaos of active construction.Instead of starting in a quiet open field, your mining operation begins inside the foundation pit of a construction project. It’s dusty, messy and surrounded by signs of a city growing upward.Underground, this area is filled with risky gas pipes that make digging more dangerous and more interesting.But beneath all that concrete, there is also a lot of mineral potential. Years of untouched soil below have created a rich digging area full of valuable resources waiting to be extracted.More to ComeThis is only the beginning. In future content updates, we’d love to introduce even more dig sites with their own themes, resources, and surprises.Also, there is still one more property in the launch lineup keeping its secrets for now. 👀Make sure to wishlist the game on Steam so you don’t miss the launch. 💙 Thank you for taking the time to read our devlog, we can’t wait to see how you build your mining empire when Ore Factory Squad launches on July 16!💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, and be part of the community from the beginning or just say hi!

newsitems.title: Next Fest Update | Demo Patch Notes - v1.6.1
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️We’re excited to announce that Ore Factory Squad is launching on July 16! With different maps to dig through, many new machines, resources, and products to produce including some of the most requested ores like Copper, Iron, Gold, and even Diamond along with many more features to try out!Make sure to wishlist the game so you don’t miss the launch!As we mentioned in our last devlog, we wanted to update the demo for Steam Next Fest and give you a closer look at what you can expect from the full game!Here’s what’s new in this updated demo:🔼 Play Beyond Level 3You can now continue playing even after reaching Level 3. The game will no longer kick you out once you reach the demo level limit.You still won’t gain new XP or progress further after Level 3, but you can keep playing, free roam around the new city, continue digging, produce more items, test the updated systems, and spend more time with your factory before the full release.⚠️ We highly recommend starting a new save for this updated demo since older saves may not display placed machines🏙️ Redstone CityIf you read our last devlog, you already know that we’ve been working on a new town called Redstone City In this updated demo, you can now freely explore part of Redstone City. You can drive around the city, explore new areas, and get a taste of how Redstone CityA traffic system has also been added. You can now see vehicles roaming around the city, making the streets feel more alive.You can also visit the bar and buy some drinks for yourself.We’ve also added a city map. Press M to open the map and check what’s going on around the city. You can see important places, your friends, and vehicles through this map.🚚 Vehicles & Car DealershipYour starter vehicle has been changed to the Light Flatbed.The Car Dealership has also been added. You can now visit it and purchase multiple vehicles.This includes utility vehicles for your work, as well as a new compact Golf Vehicle friend for getting around the city more easily.🛠 Other Changes & ImprovementsYou can see noticeable visual improvements, including updated color grading and contrast compared to the old versions.You can now build Modular Mining Lift underground. These lifts can be used for both material transfer and vertical movement, helping you bring materials up more easily and climb back to the surface using ladder sections.You can now place different types of Placeable Lights underground, including construction lights, flood lights, and hanging lamps for cave-like areas. These lights should help you organize your mining zones better and make underground work feel much clearer, especially in deeper sections.Factory Customization has been added, giving you more control over how your workspace looks and feels. You can customize your factory floor, walls, office interior design, and use new decoration items from the shop to place them wherever you want.An Emote System has been added, allowing you to express yourself in-game with different emotes while playing with friends.New settings options have been added.Many improvements have been made to the digging system, including how ore nodes behave when they are floating in the air or affected by dynamite explosions.Many improvements have been made across almost every core system in the game, including player movement, fall damage system, upgrades, UI behavior, and general gameplay feel.Several issues from the old demo have now been fixed, improved, or reworked based on your feedback.We’ll be posting a new devlog next week, where we’ll talk more about new properties and resources you’ll be digging up in the full game.Make sure to wishlist the game so you don’t miss the launch!See you during Steam Next Fest, and thank you for being part of the journey from the beginning! 💙💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, be part of the community from the beginning, or just say hi!

newsitems.title: Devlog #1 | Welcome to the Redstone City!
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️We’ve been a little quiet since the last update since we’ve been working hard on new things to show you. We’re happy to share that over 85,000 players have added the demo to their libraries, and game has passed +30,000 wishlists!We’ve also heard clearly that many of you want to experience more of the game. That’s why we’re planning to release an extended and updated version of the demo during Steam Next Fest: June 2026 featuring several new additions and improvements!We are still targeting this summer for the full release and we’ll be back with more announcements on that later. Now, let’s get into our first devlog!Welcome to the Redstone City!Redstone City is a small industrial town that was once built with big hopes, but never quite grew into the giant city it was meant to become.It has factories, warehouses, rough industrial streets, and plenty of potential. But now, the real hope of this city is you.One of the biggest pieces of feedback from the demo was that the map felt small, and that driving vehicles didn’t have much purpose yet. Expanding Redstone City was already part of our plan, and now we’re finally bringing that idea to life.In the updated version of the city, it will act as a central hub where you can visit different locations, interact with new systems, and travel to digsite from multiple exits.Of course, if we want Redstone City to feel alive, it needs more than just buildingsTraffic System & NPCsCity will feature working traffic lights and active traffic.Some vehicles will simply pass through using the highway connection, while others will drive around the city streets. These vehicles will react to you too. If you jump in front of them, don’t expect them to be happy about it. They may stop, honk, and make their opinion very clear.It’s not only vehicles either. Around key city locations, you’ll meet local NPCs who help make the city feel more grounded and alive.We’re also exploring more ways to make Redstone City feel like a lived-in place over time, but we’ll share more about that when we’re ready.So, in this living city, can you actually live a little too?Food & EntertainmentAfter a long day of digging, you can spend your evening at the bar. You’ll be able to drink different beverages, get a little tipsy, and maybe make some questionable driving decisions in-game.Please do not do that in real life!You can also visit an authentic restaurant for a proper meal, or quickly grab something from the market.Food and drinks are not just cosmetics. Each consumable has its own passive effect.For example, drinking Whiskey can warm you up and increase your mining damage for 10 minutes. Different foods and drinks will offer different bonuses, giving you more ways to prepare before heading back to workWaste Collection Center & Pawn ShopWhile digging, you can now come across Scrap and Antique items.These items may not be very useful inside your factory, but City has two different locations where you can turn them into money.At the Waste Collection Center, you can convert or sell the scrap materials you find underground and at the Pawn Shop, you can sell antique items and turn your strange underground discoveries into extra cash.Sometimes, the random junk you find can also help fund your next upgrade!Multiple Vehicle Ownership & Car DealershipIn the demo, you started with a very convenient truck with a great storage capacity but now you won’t start your brand-new factory journey with a perfect vehicle right away. Everything starts small, and grows over time.If your vehicle capacity is not enough, or if your team needs more vehicles to work efficiently, you can visit the Car Dealership and purchase new ones.The catalog includes forklifts, cargo vehicles, compact city transport options, and even a small golf cart with exactly 3 cargo slots. You’ll now be able to own multiple forklifts, trucks, and personal vehicles. Just bring enough cash, choose what fits your needs, and expand your fleetDay & NightYes, the demo already had a day and night cycle, but it has a very different feeling now.We’ve made major changes to the sky and lighting systems. Combined with updated ambient sounds, location-based city audio, and lighting that changes more dramatically throughout the day, the city now feels much more alive.Driving through the city, hearing the environment around you, seeing the lights shift, and returning to your factory after a long workday should feel much better than before!And There’s MoreThere are still many things we haven’t talked about yet, including systems like the map and navigation but we want you to discover them by yourself.And if you’re wondering what’s happening on the digging side of the game, don’t worry. In the next devlog, we’ll talk about the systems like modular mining elevator, placeable lights and new digsites!We’d love to hear what you think about these changesWhat do you like?What would you like to see more of?What kind of locations or systems would make the game more fun...

newsitems.title: Fixes & Improvements | Demo Patch Notes - v1.5.3
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️Since we released the demo last Friday, the response has been incredible.Over 45,000+ players have already added the demo to their libraries, we’ve reached 600+ concurrent players, and the game has now passed 15,000+ wishlists on Steam 🥳 If you’ve been enjoying your time with the demo, don’t forget to add the game to your wishlist on Steam! It really helps us a lot as we continue development.Here are the patch notes for today’s update:Improved loading times across:Fast travel using Bus StopsMain Menu & Save LoadingTransitions between factory and dig site with Flatbed TruckVisual improvements on various textures, including replacing some with higher-quality & refined versionsReworked the building system and controls for better stability and overall usabilityFixed an issue when placing pallets into the pallet machine with a forklift and removing them during this process causing errorsFixed filtering not working properly in the Recipes appFixed an issue where non-host players received an incorrect “active negotiation” error when trying to cancel contractsFixed an issue where non-host players received a false “contract limit reached” notification when trying to accept new contractsFixed an issue where build mode would exit brokenly after placing pallets into socketsPrevented players from clipping/teleporting behind walls when exiting vehicles by adding exit collision checksVehicle controls are now properly synced with keybindingsCamera inversion settings (horizontal/vertical) now correctly apply to vehicle camerasAdded handbrake sound effects for vehiclesUpdated the Flatbed Truck engine soundAdjusted machine output alignment to properly match sorting outputs and conveyor connectionsImproved voice chat:Player voices now come from their position inside vehiclesVoice chat icon now appears correctly while inside vehiclesFixed incorrect resource data types shown to non-host players for active propertiesReduced camera shake during crouchingAdded safeguards to prevent keybinding inputs from triggering UI actionsFixed factory logo synchronization issuesFixed an issue where placed terrain piles in the digging area would visually use the wrong layer instead of appearing as soilWe’re still actively improving the demo and carefully reading all of your feedback, it not only helps us improve the demo, but also plays a big role in shaping the full game!💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, and be part of the community from the beginning or just say hi!

newsitems.title: Fixes & Improvements | Demo Patch Notes - v1.5.2
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hi Miners! ⛏️Thank you so much to everyone who played the demo, shared feedback, and reported bugs! It’s been amazing to see the over 12,000+ players have already added the demo to their libraries 🥳 If you’ve been enjoying your time with the demo, don’t forget to add the game to your wishlist on Steam!Here are the patch notes for today’s update:Day length increased by 25%Required XP to reach Level 3 has been increased (to extend the demo duration)Fixed several issues related to carrying palletsFixed an interaction issue while a pallet is on the forkliftFixed several UI issues, including panels getting stuck on screenLocalization improvementsAdded a region filter to the Server BrowserFixed a UI issue in the Server Browser that could prevent scrollingFixed an issue that could prevent the game from saving the player’s location (both in the digsite and factory)Added a fast travel indicator to the bus stopWe’re still actively improving the demo and carefully reading all of your feedback, it not only helps us improve the demo, but also plays a big role in shaping the full game!💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, and be part of the community from the beginning or just say hi!

newsitems.title: Ore Factory Squad Demo is Out Now!
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hello Miners! ⛏️ The day is here, the Ore Factory Squad Demo is now available on Steam! If you’ve been waiting to jump into Ore Factory Squad, now is the perfect time to start digging. Play solo or with up to 4 players, buy properties, mine valuable resources, process what you extract and experience the first steps of growing your operation from a small warehouse into a industry giant! 📅 Play the Demo Now! ⭐ Wishlist the Full GameIf you like what you see, wishlist the game on Steam!Thank you for the support. We’re really excited to hear what you think about Ore Factory Squad Demo!💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, and be part of the community from the beginning or just say hi!

newsitems.title: Announcing the Ore Factory Squad ⛏️
newsitems.author: charlermaster
newsitems.contents: Hello future Miners! ⛏️Today we’re excited to officially announce Ore Factory Squad!Ore Factory Squad is a co-op mining and factory-building game with procedural mining and factory progression at its core. Play alone or with friends, buy properties, dig deep for valuable resources, complete contracts, upgrade and automate production lines, and grow your factory from zero to industry.We’re also happy to share that the demo will be available on March 27, so you won’t have to wait long to try it for yourself! 🎬 Watch the Trailer📜 More Info About The GameHere’s what you can expect in Ore Factory Squad:Procedural underground mining with fully diggable terrainUpgradeable mining tools such as shovel, pickaxe, jackhammer, and explosivesDifferent machines with different production linesFactory progression through machine unlocks, upgrades, and licensesConveyors and automationDrivable forklifts and trucks for logisticsContracts, deliveries, and stock salesCharacter and factory customizationSolo or co-op for up to 4 players ⭐ Wishlist Ore Factory SquadIf you like what you see, wishlist the game on Steam. It helps us a lot and keeps you updated as development continues!📅 Play the Demo on March 27Save the date and play the demo for free when it launches on March 27. We can’t wait to hear what you think! Thank you for the support. We’re really excited to hear what you think about Ore Factory Squad!💬 Feel free to join our Discord to follow development closely, share feedback, and be part of the community from the beginning or just say hi!
